name: W3 Legs Morning Flow
description: 15 min squat prep + hip primer. More dynamic than W1-W2 — warrior flow to open hips, crow pose attempt for fun, deeper stretches to prime the heavy squats. Your squats are going to 65/side today, so prep matters.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Deep lunge, arms overhead. Opens hip flexors before you load them with a barbell.
30s each side. From warrior II, reach over. Lateral hip + groin stretch that squats and lunges need.
Squat to standing, hands at toes. Groove the deep squat pattern before you go heavy.
Deep side lunge, alternating. Primes the same lateral hip pattern as cossack squats from KB day.
30s each side. Elbows down for deeper stretch. Hip flexors and groin.
30s each side. Internal and external hip rotation. Your hips need this before heavy squats.
NEW — try it! Hands on ground, knees on triceps, lean forward until feet lift. If you fall, you fall. It's fun and builds the body awareness you're developing from KB work. Even 3 seconds is a win.
NEW — in plank, rotate hips side to side, tapping the floor. Obliques + hip mobility in one move.
Lower back pressed down. Arms and legs hovering. 20s of pure core.

tips: 15 min — more dynamic and intense than W1-W2 leg day flows
Warrior + side angle: think yoga class energy. Breathe deep into each one.
Crow pose: just TRY it. Even if you only hold for 2 seconds, you're building something. Put a pillow in front of you if you're worried about face-planting.
This flow specifically primes heavy squats — the hip work matters today
If short on time: do dynamic warmup + hip mobility, skip core/balance
